source: MLS French test split, speaker 1406 (book 1028) + speaker 2154 (book 2576), concatenated
attribution: V. Pratap, Q. Xu, A. Sriram, G. Synnaeve, R. Collobert. MLS: A Large-Scale Multilingual Dataset for Speech Research. Interspeech 2020.
modifications: Concatenated one MLS French test utterance from speaker 1406 (1406_1028_000002, ~12.66 s) followed by 0.5 s of silence and one from speaker 2154 (2154_2576_000019, ~13.86 s), each transcoded from .opus to 16 kHz mono signed-16-bit WAV via ffmpeg, total ~27.0 s. This fills the short multi-speaker diarization gap (DER on a sub-30 s two-speaker span) for FR. A clean MLS read-speech concat is used in place of the originally-planned VoxPopuli FR clip: VoxPopuli raw archives are 5 GB+ per year and its HF segments require fragile same-session consecutive-speaker stitching, whereas the EN AMI fixture already provides real conversational multi-speaker coverage; this FR clip's role is reliable short-span DER coverage in a second language. expected_text reflects both utterances in order with first-letter capitalization and sentence punctuation applied.
expected_text: La nuit suivante, appela sa soeur quand il en fut temps. Si vous ne dormez pas, ma soeur, lui dit-elle, je vous prie, en attendant le jour qui paraîtra bientôt, de continuer le conte du pêcheur. Ils ne comprenaient point comment deux intimes, deux inséparables comme nous étions, avaient pu tout à coup devenir presque étrangers l'un à l'autre. Or, voici le secret de notre éloignement.
